title: Adaptive Retrieval Under Cost Constraints
decision: understand whether the adaptive policy improves answer quality per dollar
prompt:
Create a seven-slide research talk for applied machine-learning researchers evaluating adaptive retrieval under cost constraints. Explain the question, method, evaluation design, result, failure modes, and implications. Use the supplied experiment table exactly. Include an editable methods diagram, an editable result chart, the cost-adjusted quality formula Q divided by cost, and claim-adjacent source labels. Separate measured results from interpretation.

Design direction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

attachments.content: The adaptive policy predicts whether a query requires retrieval, retrieves at most three passages, then answers with claim-linked citations. Reported values are means over the fixed benchmark fixture; no statistical-significance claim is authorized.

designDirection.label: Evidence editorial
designDirection.instruction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

trace.summary: The user consented to send the full brief and 2 uploaded data sources to OpenRouter. The named Kimi K3 model supplied the narrative plan through pi-ai; NodeSlide normalized, persisted, and validated the deck deterministically. Self-critique: 1 pass, clean.
trace.metrics: RUN TIME 1s TOKENS 103 → 1,731 COST $0.0047 VALIDATION Passed
